contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम्
ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्म समाधिना
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmārpaṇaṃ brahma havirbrahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam
brahmaiva tena gantavyaṃ brahmakarma samādhinā
contentBlocks.translation: Brahman is the offering (the ritual or act of offering), Brahman is the clarified butter, Brahman is the sacrificial fire, and Brahman is the one offering to the fire. By realising Brahman in action, one reaches Brahman alone.
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्रह्मार्पणं (brahmārpaṇam) – Brahman is the offering (brahma:Brahman | arpaṇam:offering); ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman; हविः (havis) – clarified butter; ब्रह्माग्नौ (brahmāgnau) – in the fire of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | agnau:in the fire); ब्रह्मणा (brahmaṇā) – by Brahman; हुतम् (hutam) – offered; ब्रह्मैव (brahmaiva) – Brahman alone (brahma:Brahman | eva:alone); तेन (tena) – by that; गन्तव्यम् (gantavyam) – to be reached; ब्रह्मकर्म (brahmakarma) – action of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | karma:action); समाधिना (samādhinā) – with concentration;
